title: Quaternions
description: Master Quaternions, Euler angles, gimbal lock limitations, and SLERP interpolation for smooth rotations.
learningObjectives: Identify gimbal lock problems in Euler angle rotation setups
Define Quaternions as four-dimensional coordinates (x, y, z, w)
Use Spherical Linear Interpolation (SLERP) for smooth camera rotations

sections.title: Euler Angles vs Quaternions
sections.content:
Euler angles represent rotations via Pitch, Yaw, and Roll. While intuitive, rotating around sequential axes can cause two axes to align, locking them together and losing a degree of freedom (Gimbal Lock). 

Quaternions solve this by representing rotations in 4D space as `(x, y, z, w)` where `(x, y, z)` is the rotation axis scaled by `sin(θ/2)` and `w` is `cos(θ/2)`. Quaternions never suffer from gimbal lock and enable Spherical Linear Interpolation (SLERP) to blend between rotations smoothly along the shortest arc.

sections.title: Smooth Camera Blends via SLERP
